docs: https://developer.statuspage.io/ (Redoc-rendered OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec embedded in the page as __redoc_state.spec.data)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: full documented Statuspage API v1 surface (112 method+path endpoints across pages, components, incidents, subscribers, component groups, metrics, metrics providers, page access groups/users, incident templates, organizations) reviewed against the real embedded OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec; 10 GET list resources implemented as streams; ALL mutation endpoints are BLOCKED (ENGINE_GAP, see docs.md) because the API requires a Rails-style nested params envelope (e.g. POST /incidents expects {"incident": {...}}) that the current declarative write dialect cannot express without a new hook package, which this pass is not authorized to create -- capabilities.write remains false

excluded.reason: organization-level user/permission listing requires an organization-admin-scoped API key, a different credential class/scope than the page-scoped Console API key this bundle's spec.json documents

organization-level user/permission management requires an organization-admin-scoped API key (a different credential class/scope than the page-scoped Console API key this bundle's spec.json documents), and is additionally subject to the same params-envelope gap as ordinary CRUD

single-page GET returning the same fields the /pages list stream already reads for every page on the account; a convenience single-record fetch, not distinct catalog data

ENGINE_GAP (blocker filed, see docs.md Known limits): Statuspage mutation endpoints require a Rails-style nested top-level params envelope (e.g. POST /incidents expects {"incident": {...}}, not the record's fields flat) that the declarative write dialect cannot express: write.go's buildJSONBody/buildForm always send either every non-path-field record key flat, or an explicit body_fields allow-list flat -- there is no mechanism to additionally nest the resulting object under a fixed literal top-level key. Implementing this correctly needs either a new `body_envelope`/`wrap_key` writes.json field (Tier-1 dialect extension) or a WriteHook (Tier-2) with a single wrap-and-forward line; per this pass's constraints no new hook package may be created for statuspage, so every mutation on this API is blocked pending an engine dialect extension or an orchestrator-approved hook addition. See docs.md Known limits.
